channel.title: UFO Religion on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in UFO Religion on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Ashtar
item.description:
Ashtar, sometimes called Ashtar Sheran, is the name given to an extraterrestrial being or group of beings that some people claim to have channeled. George Van Tassel was the first to claim receiving an Ashtar message in 1952, and after his introduction, other mediums also claimed contact. These messages often included predictions, such as imminent landings of spaceships on Earth, which ultimately failed to materialize. Ashtar is often depicted as a humanoid with blond hair and European features, and the Ashtar movement is studied as a prominent form of UFO religion.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ashtar_Galactic_Command

item.title: Unarius Academy of Science
item.description:
Founded in 1954 in Los Angeles by Ernest and Ruth Norman, the Unarius Academy of Science is a non-profit organization that promotes an “interdimensional science of life” based on reincarnation and contact with extraterrestrial intelligences. The group, which relocated to El Cajon, California, in 1975, gained public notoriety through its prolific publication of channeled texts and its use of public-access television to promote beliefs in ancient interplanetary civilizations. While the organization prefers to be classified as a scientific educational foundation, sociologists and researchers identify it as a “UFO religion” due to its core tenets, such as the predicted arrival of a space-fleet. Following the deaths of its founders and subsequent leaders, including Charles Louis Spiegel, the group has continued to operate through a council-led structure despite the failure of their specific 2001 millenarian landing prophecy.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unarius_Academy_of_Science

item.title: Pana Wave
item.description:
The Pana-Wave Laboratory is a Tokyo-based division of the Japanese new religious group Chino-Shoho, which was founded by Yuko Chino in 1977. Academics and observers have classified the organization as a contactee or UFO religion that incorporates a blend of Buddhist, Christian, and New Age doctrines. While membership estimates vary, the group has historically maintained a small following, ranging from several hundred to approximately 1,200 individuals.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pana_Wave

item.title: Siderella
item.description:
Siderella was a French UFO religion founded in 1966 in Paris by Jean-Paul Appel, which drew on New Age, Buddhist, and Tantric beliefs. The group, known for its frequent name changes and communal lifestyle, centered on the anticipation of an imminent apocalypse where members would be rescued by extraterrestrials. Following allegations of abusive sexual practices and financial exploitation, the organization was formally listed as a cult in a 1995 report by the Parliamentary Commission on Cults in France. Notable members included comic book artists Jean Giraud and Sergio Macedo, the latter of whom collaborated with Appel on the 1982 comic book Timeless Voyage to document the group’s beliefs.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Siderella

item.title: Valley of the Dawn
item.description:
The Valley of the Dawn, or Vale do Amanhecer, is a new religious movement and UFO religion founded in Brazil in the 1960s. It was established in 1969 by Tia Neiva, a medium who received guidance from a spirit named Pai Seta Branca. The movement incorporates elements of Christianity, Spiritism, Umbanda, UFO beliefs, and esoteric traditions. Its main temple is located in Planaltina, Federal District, Brazil, and the community includes residents, directors, and mediums who perform daily spirit-healing rituals.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valley_of_the_Dawn

item.title: Christ Brotherhood
item.description:
The Christ Brotherhood, Inc. was a UFO religious group founded on December 16, 1956, in Orange, California, by Wallace C. Halsey. Halsey claimed to channel extraterrestrial messages warning of world destruction and the salvation of a remnant of humanity. The group began constructing a compound with underground bomb shelters in Cache Valley, Utah, in 1961, intended to house about 1000 people. The organization lost cohesion after Halsey disappeared in a plane crash in March 1963, though his teachings were later published posthumously.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christ_Brotherhood

item.title: Fiat Lux
item.description:
Fiat Lux is a neo-revelationist UFO religion founded in 1980 by Erika Bertschinger-Eicke, who was known as Uriella. Members of the group, primarily located in Germany’s Black Forest, believe in the imminent apocalypse and the arrival of alien ships to evacuate adherents to a new paradise called “Amora.” Uriella, who claimed to receive divine messages, died in 2019. The organization has been described as a cult and has an estimated following of thousands.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fiat_Lux_%28UFO_religion%29

item.title: Chen Tao (UFO religion)
item.description:
Chen Tao, also known as God’s Salvation Church, was a UFO religion founded in Taiwan in 1996 by Hon-Ming Chen. The group relocated to Texas in 1997 and gained notoriety for a failed millennial prophecy. Chen Tao members wore white clothing and cowboy hats, and called their leader “Teacher Chen.” After their prophecy of God’s appearance did not occur, the group largely disbanded, with many members returning to Taiwan.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chen_Tao_%28UFO_religion%29
